results.rendered_html: pravrajyā (wie eben) f. Auswanderung, das Ausziehen in die Fremde  MBH. 5,3186 .  R. 6,8,27 . pravrajyāyaiva wohl zusammengezogen aus pravrajyāyā eva  MBH. 4,533 . aber apravrajye (neutr.) 5,783 . das Wandern —, der Stand des frommen Bettlers  H. 81 , Sch. pravrajyāsu tiṣṭhatām  M. 5,89 . sg.  MBH. 3,16007 . 5,6029 .  KUMĀRAS. 6,6 . bauddhānāṃ pravrajyorjitatejasām  RĀJA-TAR. 1,171 .  PAÑCAT. ed. orn. 57,4 .  VAJRAS. 222,7 . pravrajyāvasita  YĀJÑ. 2,183 .  MIT. 268,3 . 12 .  MED. t. 188 . ˚vastu  VYUTP. 211 .

results.rendered_html:
pravrajyā
1 Going abroad, migration.
2 Roaming, wandering about as a religious mendicant;  Māl. 4. 6. 
3 The order of a religious mendicant, a mendicant's life, the fourth (or bhikṣu) order in the religious life of a Brāhmaṇa; pravrajyāṃ kalpavṛkṣā ivāśritāḥ  Ku. 6. 6  (where  Malli.  says pravrajyā means the vānaprastha or third order).
Comp.
— avasitaḥ a religious mendicant who renounces his order.
